CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-19-2026 Set For Trial On Nov-16-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-17-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HANNA KHATKEVICH'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Artsiom Khatkevich, in person, Plaintiff in propria persona.
No other appearances by any interested party.

The interpreter stated for the record his name is Vitaliy Denyson, Certified Russian Interpreter, Cert. #: 301807, assisting Defendant Hanna Khatkevich. The language to be interpreted is Russian to English and English to Russian.

Not argued/contested. There are multiple attempts to call this matter with the assistance of the Russian Interpreter with no appearance by the Defendant. Thus, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Hanna Khatkevich's anti-SLAPP motion to strike both claims alleged in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Artsiom Khatkevich is denied as to both claims. Artsiom's request for fees and costs is denied. Assuming without deciding that Hanna satisfied her burden of showing that Artsiom's two claims arise from activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute, Artsiom has shown that neither of his claims are barred by the litigation privilege and both of the claims have minimal merit, thereby satisfying his burden of showing that his claims have the requisite probability of being proven and requiring that the motion must be denied. The claims are not barred by the litigation privilege because most or all of the allegations of wrongdoing by Hanna occurred outside of or in anticipation of litigation and either pre-dated Hanna's filing of an application for a domestic violence restraining order or post-dated the decision on her restraining order application. The declarations submitted by Artsiom show that he has admissible evidence showing that most or all of the conduct attributed to Hanna that is the basis for Artsiom's claims did in fact occur. Artsiom is not awarded fees or costs in opposing this motion because his opposition papers were not filed by an attorney and the motion was neither frivolous nor brought for an improper purpose.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Dec-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Artsiom Khatkevich, 415-961-1342 Plantiff in Pro Per (Court)
Hanna Khatkevich, 415-335-0404 Defendant in Pro Per (Court)
Andrew Derbentsev-Crawford, #514119 Court Certified Russian Interperter for Pro Per Defendant Hanna Khatkevich

Matter called at 11:30 a.m. to allow for Court Certified Russian Interperter.

Defendant agrees to electronic service. Email for service is provided in open court. Defendant argues that Plaintiff's opposition was improperly served. Plaintiff advises the court of his efforts to properly serve his opposition. All parties are present. Court allows argument.

Hearing held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HANNA KHATKEVICH'S Motion To Dismiss.

Defendant's Hannah Khatekevich's Motion to Dismiss is SUSTAINED as to CoA 2 and OVERRULED as to CoA 1.

(see word minutes for complete ruling)

Proposed order is submitted.
